task_completion.description: The diagram includes all six main carbon molecules represented as rectangles or rounded rectangles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 6 distinct nodes shaped as rectangles or rounded rectangles containing the names: Glucose, Glucose-6-Phosphate, Fructose-6-Phosphate, Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate, Dihydroxyacetone Phosphate, and Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate.

task_completion.description: The diagram includes arrows connecting the molecules, with the five specific enzymes labeled on or near the arrows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for arrows indicating the reaction sequence. The arrows must be labeled with the text: Hexokinase, Phosphoglucose Isomerase, Phosphofructokinase, Aldolase, and Triose Phosphate Isomerase.

task_completion.description: ATP consumption and ADP production are explicitly labeled where applicable in the pathway.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'ATP' and 'ADP' text near the reactions catalyzed by Hexokinase (Step 1) and Phosphofructokinase (Step 3), indicating ATP is being consumed and ADP is being produced.

task_completion.description: The diagram explicitly represents the split of Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate into two different molecules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a branching point in the diagram where the node for Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate has arrows pointing to two separate nodes: Dihydroxyacetone Phosphate and Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate.

task_completion.description: The source file is saved as 'glycolysis.drawio' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the file manager, desktop icons, or terminal output in the screenshots to verify the existence of a file named 'glycolysis.drawio' on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as 'glycolysis.png' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the file manager, desktop icons, or an image viewer in the screenshots to verify the existence of a file named 'glycolysis.png' on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical user interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the draw.io application open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI by using a script (e.g., Python, Graphviz) to generate the drawio or png files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows showing scripts being executed to generate XML or image files directly from the text file.

integrity.description: The exported PNG matches the diagram created in the draw.io GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual layout, shapes, and text in the draw.io canvas screenshots should perfectly match the final exported 'glycolysis.png' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The energy investment phase of glycolysis (Steps 1-5) involves 6 molecules: Glucose, Glucose-6-Phosphate, Fructose-6-Phosphate, Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate, Dihydroxyacetone Phosphate, and Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate. These are processed sequentially by 5 enzymes: Hexokinase, Phosphoglucose Isomerase, Phosphofructokinase, Aldolase, and Triose Phosphate Isomerase. ATP is consumed (converted to ADP) at two steps: Step 1 (Hexokinase) and Step 3 (Phosphofructokinase). At Step 4, Fructose-1,6-Bisphosphate is cleaved by Aldolase into two distinct molecules: Dihydroxyacetone Phosphate and Glyceraldehyde-3-Phosphate.
